summary: Osteosarcoma is the most commonly diagnosed primary bone cancer in children, accounting for approximately 4% of all pediatric bone tumors and consistently showing a higher prevalence than other types such as Ewing’s sarcoma across various studies emphasizing its unique tendency toward younger age groups affected primarily during adolescence or early adulthood stages development growth spurts occurring physiologically biologically driven hormonally influenced genetically predisposed factors playing crucial roles influencing susceptibility risk profile disease progression outcomes
